title: Cherry-Pick a Range Without Merge Baggage
description: A long-running `feat/v2` branch has ten commits, but only three of them (a specific bug fix and two tests) are ready for production and must be applied to `main` without bringing in the rest of the unfinished v2 work.
objective: The branch `main` must contain exactly the three specified commits from `feat/v2` (applied as new commits via cherry-pick). The rest of the v2 commits must not be on `main`. The working tree must be clean.
hints: Identify the SHAs of the three commits using `git log feat/v2 --oneline`.
`git cherry-pick <sha1> <sha2> <sha3>` applies multiple commits in one command.
If there are conflicts during cherry-pick, resolve them the same way as a merge conflict, then `git cherry-pick --continue`.
